data.title: XenDex Extends $XDX Token Presale Amid Ongoing Exchange Listing Discussions
data.description:
SYDNEY, May 29, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — With over 90% of the $XDX presale allocation already sold, this final window, which is just about a 48-hour grace period, is the last chance for late investors to buy the token at discounted presale rates. Once this short extension ends, no further grace will be granted, and $XDX will launch at market price across multiple exchanges.

The presale extension coincides with bullish developments within the broader XRP ecosystem. Ripple has announced its acquisition of Circle, the issuer of USDC, while Volatility Shares has launched the XRPI Futures ETF, both of which have sent positive shockwaves across the crypto market. As institutional interest in XRP surges, XenDex is riding that momentum as the most anticipated DeFi platform to emerge on XRPL.

What Makes XenDex Unique?

XenDex is the first fully integrated decentralized exchange on XRP Ledger. Unlike conventional DEXs, XenDex merges multiple financial tools into a single interface:

  • AI Copy Trading: Automatically mimic the trades of top-performing traders and minimize loss.
  • Lending & Borrowing: Lend or borrow assets directly without third-party interference.
  • Cross-Chain Swaps: Trade XRP assets across other blockchains like; Ethereum, BNB, Solana, and more.
  • DAO Governance: $XDX holders can vote on major protocol upgrades and decisions.

Why Buy $XDX Now?

Aside from the deeply discounted price, early holders of $XDX gain access to:

  • Staking and yield farming rewards
  • Reduced fees on all trading, lending, borrowing and other DeFi functions
  • Priority access to new features, listings, and platform upgrades
  • Governance voting rights that give users control over the platform’s future upgrades and decisions

XenDex Presale

Crypto analysts are already speculating a strong pump once $XDX gets listed on the exchanges, and the listing process is currently in negotiation. These major exchanges include; Binance, BitMart, Gate.io, MagneticX, FirstLedger, and more.

How To Buy $XDX During The Presale?

  • Visit the official presale link: https://xendex.net/presale
  • Set up a Trustline using an XRP-compatible wallet (e.g., Xaman)
  • Minimum buy is 150 XRP
  • Exchange rate is 1.25 XRP = 10 XDX
  • Full guide available here: https://xdxdocs.gitbook.io/xendex/buy-usdxdx-token-presale

XenDex Presale Details

  • Soft Cap: Reached
  • Hard Cap: 93% SoldTime Left: 72-Hour Grace Period Only
  • Presale Rate: 150 XRP = 1200 $XDX

Buy XDX At A Discount Before Pumping Upon Listing on Exchanges: https://xendex.net/presale

Website: https://xendex.net

data.title: FlexShopper Collaborates With Icon Vehicle Dynamics to Make Off-Road Vehicle Upgrades Affordable
data.description:
FlexShopper said late Thursday it entered into a partnership with Icon Vehicle Dynamics to offer its lease-to-own financing services to Icon's clients, making vehicle upgrades more feasible.

Under the agreement, Icon customers can now acquire off-road performance vehicle parts and accessories with flexible weekly payments through a streamlined approval process, the company said.

data.title: Wall Street ends up with Nvidia, appeals court reinstates Trump tariffs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Best Buy slumps after cutting annual sales, profit forecasts
  • Boeing climbs as CEO aims to boost 737 MAX production
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.3%, S&P 500 up 0.4%, Nasdaq up 0.4%

U.S. stocks ended higher on Thursday as shares of Nvidia gained after its quarterly results, while investors digested a late-afternoon court ruling that reinstated the most sweeping of President Donald Trump's tariffs.

The appeals court ruling came a day after a trade court had ordered an immediate block on the tariffs.

Trading was choppy for much of the day and indexes ended well off their highs of the session, however, with investors trying to digest the rulings and as shares of Salesforce fell 3.3%. Salesforce's stock was down even as the enterprise software provider raised its annual revenue and adjusted profit forecasts.

"Trump has already rolled back most of these tariffs anyway, so these court rulings are just headlines," said Adam Sarhan, chief executive of 50 Park Investments in New York.

"As long as the market doesn't tank on the news, it's just a secondary" thing, he said.

Nvidia gained 3.2% after the company late on Wednesday reported upbeat sales results, driven by customers stockpiling AI chips ahead of U.S. export restrictions on China.

The company, however, warned that the new curbs are expected to cut $8 billion from its current-quarter sales.

Optimism about corporate earnings and Nvidia in particular is providing some support, said Oliver Pursche, senior vice president, adviser for Wealthspire Advisors in Westport, Connecticut.

"It's about corporate earnings in general," he said.

Nvidia, which is now up just 3.6% for the year, was the last of the "Magnificent Seven" megacap tech and growth companies to report results for this earnings period.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 117.03 points, or 0.28%, to 42,215.73, the S&P 500 gained 23.62 points, or 0.40%, to 5,912.17 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 74.93 points, or 0.39%, to 19,175.87.

Trade developments have whipsawed the stock market this year, especially after Trump's April 2 announcement of sweeping tariffs on imports globally.

The S&P 500 has rebounded from a selloff in early April as trade tensions have eased and as first-quarter earnings have been mostly better than expected. The index is now up 0.5% for 2025 but off its February record high.

Still, investors have become accustomed to Trump announcing steep tariffs, only to postpone them soon afterward. That has led to the acronym TACO (Trump Always Chickens Out), coined by the Financial Times.

"It's cute; it's not a strategy," said Pursche, referring to the acronym.

"However, from a purely American business perspective, there have been incremental gains achieved by the Trump administration on trade, and that shouldn't be ignored."

Boeing rose 3.3% after CEO Kelly Ortberg said the planemaker aims to increase production of its best-selling 737 MAX jets to 42 aircraft per month in the next few months and boost output to 47 a month in early 2026.

On the economic front, a second reading from the Commerce Department showed gross domestic product contracted 0.2% in the first quarter. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast a 0.3% contraction.

In other earnings-related news, Best Buy shares fell 7.3% after the electronics retailer lowered its annual comparable sales and profit forecasts amid concerns that U.S. tariffs would weigh on consumer demand for big-ticket items.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 2.26-to-1 ratio on the NYSE. There were 114 new highs and 35 new lows on the NYSE.

On the Nasdaq, 2,673 stocks rose and 1,806 fell as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.48-to-1 ratio.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 18.65 billion shares, compared with the 17.7 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

data.title: Bombardier's First Global 8000 Production Jet Completes Successful Maiden Flight
data.description:
Bombardier (BBD-B.TO) on Thursday said the first production Global 8000 aircraft completed its maiden flight on May 16 from the company's Aircraft Assembly Centre in Mississauga, Ontario.

During the flight, the aircraft underwent a series of standard production tests, and all systems performed as expected, the company added.

The aircraft will next head to Bombardier's Laurent Beaudoin Completion Centre in Montreal, where its interior will be completed. The Global 8000 is expected to enter service in the second half of 2025, the company said.

Also on Thursday, the company said it closed a US$500-million offering of notes. The Senior Notes due June, 2033, carry an interest rate of 6.75% and were sold at par. Proceeds will be used to repay existing debt.

The company's Class B shares closed up $3.35 to $95.15 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: Zeo Energy Gets Noncompliance Notice From Nasdaq
data.description:
Zeo Energy said late Thursday it received a non-compliance notice from Nasdaq for failing to file quarterly report on Form 10-Q with the Securities and Exchange Commission by May 15.

The company said it has until June 16 to submit a plan to regain compliance.

The shares were down more than 13% in after-hours activity.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Tariff Ruling Is Put On Hold While Trump Administration Appeals | Trump ...
data.description:
Tariff Ruling Is Put On Hold While Trump Administration Appeals

A federal court pauses the decision that had invalidated the president's sweeping levies.

----

Trump Tells Powell He Is Making a Mistake by Not Cutting Interest Rates

The president and the Federal Reserve chair meet for the first time in Trump's second term.

----

Tariff Ruling Is a Setback for Trump but Doesn't End Trade War

Trade experts say the president is unlikely to abandon levies as the principal tool in his quest to rewrite the rules of global commerce.

----

Stocks Edge Higher; Court Stays Ruling Blocking Trump's Tariffs

Stocks settled higher after an appeals court stayed an earlier decision to strike down President Trump's most sweeping tariffs while it considers the administration's challenge to it.

----

Interest-Rate Cuts Could Resume with Trade Deals, Says Chicago Fed President

Austan Goolsbee told a conference that while the economy is strong, that strength could erode if uncertainty lingers.

----

U.S. GDP Shrank Less Than First Reported Last Quarter. This Is Why.

The Bureau of Economic Analysis released its second estimate for first-quarter GDP growth Thursday morning.

----

Pending Home Sales Slump as Mortgage Prices Weigh

The number of homes going under contract in the U.S. fell sharply in April, as high mortgage prices continue to hamper the market, according to a monthly index.

----

Bank of Mexico Minutes Signal Another Half-Point Rate Cut

The central bank lowered its benchmark interest-rate target to 8.5% from 9% on May 15.

----

U.S. Jobless Claims Rose Last Week

In the week through May 24, 240,000 people newly filed for unemployment benefits, compared with 226,000 the week prior and economists' expectations of 230,000 claims.

----

Returning to More Open Trade Calls for Hard Work, Says BOE's Bailey

The head of the Bank of England says strains in the global trading system that have most recently been manifested in higher U.S. tariffs make life difficult for central bankers.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 2.8 million barrels, exceeding estimates for a drop of 600,000 barrels.

----

South African Reserve Bank Cuts Main Repo Rate to 7.25%

The South African Reserve Bank cut its main repo rate by 25 basis points to 7.25% on Thursday, as tensions with the U.S. continue amid ongoing global uncertainty.

----

Bank of Korea Cuts Rates, Slashes 2025 Growth Outlook

South Korea's central bank cut its policy rate in a widely expected move to support the country's sagging economy.

----

University Endowments Are Breaking Up With Private Equity. It's Hard to Do.

It once seemed like the perfect investment-offering better returns than stocks with less risk. Lately, though, private equity has become an albatross.

----

One of the best-performing stock markets of 2025 is facing a big risk this weekend

The outcome of Sunday's presidential vote is more uncertain than investors had hoped.

data.description:
Costco Logs Higher Profit, Sales Despite Declining Consumer Confidence

The warehouse-club chain reported same-store sales rose 5.7%. Analysts projected comparable sales to climb 6%.

----

Dell Technologies Raises Outlook as Sales Rise on AI Demand

The maker of computers and software on Thursday said it now expects adjusted earnings per share of $9.40, up from its previous outlook for $9.30.

----

Boeing Stock Jumps on CEO Comments. Here's What He Said.

Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg has positive news about the production rates, 737 MAX variants, and China. Investors look pleased.

----

Synopsys Confirms China Export Ban. The Stock Drops.

----

Meta Fired Palmer Luckey. Now, They're Teaming Up on a Defense Contract.

The Facebook and Instagram parent is partnering with Luckey's Anduril Industries to develop combat VR headsets for the Army.

----

Ulta Beauty Raises Full-Year Outlook After Results Beat Estimates

Same-store sales increased 2.9%, beating the 0.2% bump Wall Street was anticipating. An increase in average ticket and the number of transactions helped drive same-store-sales growth, Ulta said.

----

Ben & Jerry's Calls Gaza Conflict a Genocide, Putting Unilever In A Tough Spot

Ben & Jerry's independent board plans to post a statement expressing solidarity with protesters against deaths of Palestinians in Gaza.

----

Newark Airfares Will Be a Steal This Summer, United CEO Says

The airline is slashing fares to coax wary fliers to the airport after flight disruptions and safety fears.

Retailers Cheer Trade-Court Surprise, but Stick to Their Plans

----

Ex-Goldman Banker Sentenced to Two Years in 1MDB Bribery Scandal

----

Marvell Earnings Are Solid but Not Enough for Wall Street

----

Kohl's Results Top Prior Forecast; 2025 Outlook Maintained

Kohl's posted first-quarter results slightly ahead of its previous expectations, a bright spot for a company facing a difficult operating environment and recently ousted leadership.

----

Hormel Foods Trims Guidance, Citing Tariff Impact

Hormel Foods trimmed its earnings outlook for the year, which factors in the impact of tariffs and ongoing momentum of brands such as Planters peanuts and its turkey product.

----

Best Buy Cuts Guidance as Tariff Question Looms

----

Senators Bash Nvidia's Plans for Facility in China

Sen. Jim Banks and Sen. Elizabeth Warren are accusing the chip maker of undercutting national security.

data.title: Hudbay Provides an Update on the Wildfire Situation near Flin Flon
data.description:
TORONTO, May 29, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Hudbay Minerals Inc. (“Hudbay” or the “Company”) (TSX, NYSE: HBM) is responding to the evolving wildfire situation in northern Manitoba and is working closely with local authorities to ensure its employees remain safe as well as comply with the regional wildfire evacuation order that includes Flin Flon and the surrounding areas. Only essential Hudbay personnel, authorized by emergency services, will remain in Flin Flon to assist with emergency activities. While Hudbay no longer has active mine production activities in Flin Flon after the closure of the 777 mine in 2022, the Company has ongoing care and maintenance activities in Flin Flon as well as services to support the Snow Lake operations, including concentrate handling, fabrication shops and administration. The Flin Flon community has been an important part of Hudbay’s operations for nearly 100 years.

Hudbay continues to maintain operations in Snow Lake, which is located approximately 200 kilometres east of Flin Flon. The Company expects temporary reduced production levels in Snow Lake as a large portion of its workforce resides in Flin Flon. However, given the strong performance in Snow Lake year-to-date, the Company remains well-positioned to achieve its annual guidance metrics for 2025. Hudbay has paused exploration activities in the Flin Flon and Snow Lake region as fire mitigation efforts are underway.

“The safety of our employees, their families and the communities we serve is our top priority,” said Rob Carter, Vice President of Hudbay’s Manitoba Business Unit. “We are collaborating closely with the City of Flin Flon, local communities and provincial authorities to provide support during this challenging time. We will continue to monitor the situation, ensuring a safe return to full operations as soon as it is advisable.”

As part of its commitment to support Manitoba and the Flin Flon community, Hudbay is taking the following actions:

  • Securing additional accommodations in Snow Lake for its evacuated employees and their families.
  • Deploying trained emergency personnel to aid firefighting efforts.
  • Maintaining communication with the City of Flin Flon, local communities and provincial authorities about the resources Hudbay has available to support emergency response efforts, such as supplies, equipment and water trucks.
  • Providing facility infrastructure information to assist with planning and response.

Forward-Looking Information

This news release contains “forward-looking statements” and “forward-looking information” (collectively, “forward-looking information”) within the meaning of applicable Canadian and United States securities legislation. Forward- looking information includes information that relates to, among other things, statements with respect to potential impacts from wildfires on the Company’s operations in Manitoba, including the ability to achieve guidance expectations. Forward-looking information is not, and cannot be, a guarantee of future results or events.

Forward-looking information is based on, among other things, opinions, assumptions, estimates and analyses that, while considered reasonable by Hudbay at the date the forward-looking information is provided, inherently are subject to significant risks, uncertainties, contingencies and other factors that may cause actual results and events to be materially different from those expressed or implied by the forward-looking information. Should one or more risk, uncertainty, contingency or other factor materialize or should any factor or assumption prove incorrect, actual results could vary materially from those expressed or implied in the forward-looking information. Hudbay does not assume any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking information after the date of this news release or to explain any material difference between subsequent actual events and any forward-looking information, except as required by applicable law.

About Hudbay

Hudbay (TSX, NYSE: HBM) is a copper-focused critical minerals mining company with three long-life operations and a world-class pipeline of copper growth projects in tier-one mining-friendly jurisdictions of Canada, Peru and the United States.

Hudbay’s operating portfolio includes the Constancia mine in Cusco (Peru), the Snow Lake operations in Manitoba (Canada) and the Copper Mountain mine in British Columbia (Canada). Copper is the primary metal produced by the company, which is complemented by meaningful gold production and by-product zinc, silver and molybdenum. Hudbay’s growth pipeline includes the Copper World project in Arizona (United States), the Mason project in Nevada (United States), the Llaguen project in La Libertad (Peru) and several expansion and exploration opportunities near its existing operations.

The value Hudbay creates and the impact it has is embodied in its purpose statement: “We care about our people, our communities and our planet. Hudbay provides the metals the world needs. We work sustainably, transform lives and create better futures for communities.” Hudbay’s mission is to create sustainable value and strong returns by leveraging its core strengths in community relations, focused exploration, mine development and efficient operations.

For further information, please contact:

Candace Brûlé

Vice President, Investor Relations, Financial Analysis and External Communications

(416) 814-4387

investor.relations@hudbay.com

data.title: Baylin Amends and Restates Credit Agreement to 2026
data.description:
Baylin Technologies after close of trade Thursday entered into an Amended and Restated Credit Agreement with its principal lender, Royal Bank of Canada to continue a $15 million secured revolving credit facility, extending the maturity date to Jan. 31, 2026.

The original date was May 30, 2025.

Other than the addition of a minimum EBITDA covenant, the terms and conditions of the Credit Agreement remain substantially similar to those of the original credit agreement dated March 29, 2019.

"We continue to be extremely grateful to Royal Bank for its continuing strong support of our business and flexibility in recognizing our business needs," said CFO Cliff Gary, "particularly in light of the uncertain trade environment."

data.title: RBB Bancorp Gets Board Approval for $18 Million Stock Repurchase Program
data.description:
RBB Bancorp and its Royal Business Bank and RBB Asset Management units said late Thursday they received board approval to repurchase up to $18 million of outstanding common shares.

The buyback program is valid through June 30, 2026.

RBB's shares were up more than 3% in after-hours activity.

data.title: Seacoast Banking of Florida to Acquire Villages Bancorporation
data.description:
Seacoast Banking of Florida agreed to acquire Villages Bancorporation, the parent of Citizens First Bank, for $710.8 million in cash and stock.

Under the terms of the deal, each share of Villages Bancorporation common stock will be converted at closing into the right to get $1,000 in cash, 38.5000 shares of Seacoast common stock or a combination of 25% cash and 75% stock, at the shareholders' election.

The deal is expected to close in Q4, Seacoast said Thursday in a statement.

Seacoast shares rose 2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Ambarella Upgrades Outlook After Narrower Loss, Higher Revenue in 1Q
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Ambarella boosted its full-year sales outlook after logging a narrower loss and higher revenue in the first quarter.

The Santa Clara, Calif., semiconductor-design company on Thursday said it now expects revenue to rise 19% to 25% to about $348 million for the fiscal year, up from its previous outlook for mid to high teens growth. Analysts polled by FactSet expect sales of $333 million.

The broader guidance range takes into consideration an uncertain geopolitical environment, Chief Executive Fermi Wang said.

For the current quarter, the company expects sales of $86 million to $94 million, ahead of the $84.7 million expected by analysts.

The outlook came after Ambarella posted a loss of $24.3 million, or 58 cents a share, in its fiscal first quarter, compared with a loss of $37.9 million, or 93 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 7 cents, ahead of estimates of 4 cents a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose 58% to $85.9 million, above analyst estimates for $84 million.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Cooper Companies Inc reports results for the quarter ended April 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cooper Companies Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 96 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended April 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 85 cents. The mean expectation of sixteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 93 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 90 cents to 95 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 6.3% to $1.00 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $995.35 million.

  • Cooper Companies Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 44 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $87.7 million.

  • Cooper Companies Inc shares had fallen by 5.0% this quarter and lost 12.9% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.6% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 13 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the medical equipment, supplies & distribution peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cooper Companies Inc is $106.00

data.title: Symbol for Anglo American Platinum Ltd. (AMS.JO) Now VAL.JO
News On SYLA Technologies Co. Ltd. (SYT) Now Under SYTTY
NextTrip, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
NextTrip, Inc., an early-stage, technology-driven travel company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company, known for its proprietary NXT2.0 travel booking engine and integrated media properties, aims to revolutionize the travel industry by offering personalized travel experiences and efficient trip planning.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $501,423,000, an increase of 9% from the previous year, driven by the continued implementation of the booking engine and expanded product offerings.
  • Cost of Revenue: $498,121,000, an increase of 25% from the previous year, attributed to the increase in revenue.
  • Operating Expenses: $7,416,731,000, an increase of 29% from the previous year, due to higher salaries and benefits, technology expenses, and professional service fees.
  • Net Loss from Continuing Operations: $10,121,038,000, compared to a net loss of $6,656,837,000 in the previous year, with increases in both operating and other loss components.
  • Net Loss Applicable to Common Shareholders: $10,198,684,000, compared to $7,339,276,000 in the previous year, with preferred dividends of $78,600 in 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Integrated Travel Booking Platform: NextTrip's core business revolves around its proprietary NXT2.0 travel booking engine, which has been enhanced through acquisitions and integrations, including the Bookit.com platform. This engine supports a wide range of travel products and services, offering personalized experiences and efficient trip planning.
  • Media Integration: The company integrates its media properties, such as Journy.tv, Compass.tv, and Travel Magazine, with its travel booking platform to drive high-intention traffic and create a high-margin advertising revenue stream. This integration aims to inspire and empower consumers in their travel planning.
  • Product Offerings: NextTrip offers a mix of direct contracts and third-party API content across leisure and business travel, alternative lodging, wellness travel, and media solutions. Key offerings include NextTrip Vacations, NextTrip Luxury & Business, NextTrip Cruises, and NextTrip Solutions.
  • New Product Launches: In March 2025, NextTrip launched NextTrip Cruise, a fully integrated cruise booking engine providing access to over 10,000 sailings and 35 cruise partners, offering exclusive pricing and concierge services.
  • Strategic Partnerships: The company announced a partnership with Intimate Hotels of Barbados in April 2025, serving as the official booking engine for over 35 independent hotels and vacation rentals, enhancing its inventory and market reach.
  • Acquisitions: NextTrip acquired Five Star Alliance, a luxury travel agency, in February 2025, expanding its luxury travel offerings and integrating a curated collection of over 5,000 five-star hotels and resorts worldwide.
  • Future Outlook: NextTrip plans to further integrate its media features with its booking system, develop a multi-level rewards program, and introduce an AI-powered travel assistant. These initiatives aim to enhance user engagement, speed up bookings, and increase customer value.
  • Technology and Infrastructure: The company's websites are hosted using globally distributed cloud services, designed to manage traffic increases and ensure data security. This infrastructure supports the scalability and reliability of NextTrip's online marketplace.
  • Travel Agent and Groups Platforms: The Travel Agent Platform is in beta testing with over 150 agents, and the Groups Platform has been launched to cater to complex travel needs like conferences and destination weddings, tapping into underserved markets.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: NextTrip is focused on developing an integrated travel booking and media platform, leveraging its proprietary NXT2.0 booking engine. The company aims to expand its product offerings and enhance its travel and media ecosystem to drive revenue growth. Strategic acquisitions, such as the purchase of Five Star Alliance, are part of its plan to enhance luxury travel services. The company is also working on integrating its media properties to create a high-margin advertising revenue stream.
  • Capital Management: NextTrip has been financing its operations primarily through the issuance of short-term promissory notes, advances from related parties, and private placements of its securities. Recent capital management activities include entering into a securities purchase agreement with Alumni Capital LP for a short-term promissory note and warrants, and establishing a $3 million revolving line of credit with Monaco Investment Partners II, LP. The company has also raised funds through the issuance of preferred shares and related party advances. No equity repurchases were made during the reported period, and the company has not paid dividends on its common stock.
  • Future Outlook: NextTrip plans to raise additional funds through equity or debt financings to support ongoing operations and expand its market presence. The company estimates a need for a minimum of $5.5 million to continue operations over the next twelve months. There is a focus on increasing market penetration of its products and expanding the marketing and development of its travel and technology-driven offerings. The company acknowledges the substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern and is actively seeking additional financing to address this challenge.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company is heavily reliant on the global travel industry, and any prolonged decrease in travel, particularly air travel, due to health concerns, security threats, or geopolitical issues, could adversely affect its operating results.
  • Operational Risks: The company needs additional capital to continue operations, with a substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern. It has incurred losses since its inception and may never become profitable.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with varying international regulations and taxation requirements is burdensome and could harm the company's operations. Changes in laws or regulations could increase costs or discourage suppliers from partnering with NextTrip.
  • Emerging Risks: The company faces challenges in maintaining and enhancing its brand identity, expanding its traveler base internationally, and competing in a highly competitive market dominated by key players like Expedia and Booking.com.
  • Technological Risks: The success of the company's SEO and SEM strategy is uncertain due to potential changes in search engine algorithms. Additionally, the company is exposed to cybersecurity threats and system vulnerabilities that could lead to data breaches and service outages.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the need for significant investments in marketing and product development to drive growth. The company is focusing on expanding its inventory base and developing key partnerships to enhance its offerings. However, the lack of funding for marketing programs severely restricts its ability to capitalize on existing travel technology platforms.
  • Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk: NextTrip is exposed to fluctuations in currency exchange rates due to its international operations. These fluctuations may cause revenue and operating results to differ materially from expectations. The company may enter into hedging arrangements to manage foreign currency exposure, but such activities may not completely eliminate fluctuations and may be costly.

SEC Filing: NextTrip, Inc. [ NTRP ] - 10-K - May. 29, 2025

data.title: Autonomix Medical, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Autonomix Medical, Inc., a development-stage medical device company specializing in innovative technologies for sensing and treating nervous system disorders, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces as it advances its technology, particularly in the area of pancreatic cancer pain management.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $11.4 million, an improvement from the previous year's net loss of $15.4 million.
  • Loss from Operations: $11.6 million, reflecting a slight decrease in operating expenses due to cost management efforts.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $6.46, a significant reduction from the previous year's $14.82, indicating a decrease in net loss relative to the number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Autonomix is focused on developing a catheter-based microchip-enabled sensing array with high sensitivity, initially targeting pancreatic cancer pain.
  • Technology Development: The company is advancing its technology in diagnostic sensing and therapeutic radiofrequency ablation, with successful results in animal models and ongoing efforts to meet human use standards.
  • Clinical Trials: Autonomix completed the initial phase of its first-in-human proof-of-concept trial (PoC 1) for pancreatic cancer pain, showing positive results. This has led to an expanded protocol (PoC 2) to include additional visceral cancers and earlier-stage pancreatic cancer patients.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to integrate sensing and treatment in a future pivotal clinical trial, aiming for a commercial launch. They are also exploring expansion into other sectors like oncology and gastroenterology.
  • Regulatory Pathway: Autonomix anticipates using the De Novo classification request for regulatory approval, as their technology combines novel signal sensitivity and targets specific indications without existing predicates.
  • Commercialization Challenges: Scaling up from a prototype to a commercial version is a significant challenge. The current device is hand-built, and efforts are ongoing to develop a robust version for human trials.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds 86 issued patents and 41 pending applications across 18 patent families, covering various aspects of its technology.
  • Competition: The electrophysiology market is competitive, with large players like Medtronic and Boston Scientific. However, Autonomix's transvascular sensing and ablation method for pancreatic cancer pain is unique.
  • Regulatory Engagement: Autonomix is actively engaging with the FDA for U.S.-based IDE clinical trials, starting with a small EFS study leading to a larger pivotal trial.
  • Operational Focus: The company is initially focusing on pancreatic cancer pain management as a proof of concept, with potential expansion to other disorders involving the peripheral nervous system.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: Autonomix is advancing its innovative medical device technology for sensing and treating nervous system disorders, with an initial focus on pancreatic cancer pain management. The company is improving its catheter design for human use and has expanded its trial protocol following positive initial results.
  • Capital Management: The company has engaged in several capital management activities, including an At Market Issuances Sales Agreement to sell shares of common stock with an aggregate sales price of up to $2.1 million. It completed a firm commitment underwritten public offering, raising approximately $10 million in gross proceeds, and its IPO, raising $11.2 million in gross proceeds. Additionally, the company has issued convertible promissory notes and accompanying warrants as part of a bridge financing effort.
  • Future Outlook: Autonomix estimates that its current cash resources are sufficient to fund operations into the first calendar quarter of 2026. The company anticipates needing to raise an additional $32 to $40 million to fund operations through the initial commercial launch of its technology. The company plans to continue its clinical trials and product development efforts, with a focus on obtaining regulatory clearance and commercializing its products.

Challenges and Risks

  • Going Concern: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to an accumulated deficit of $50.4 million and negative cash flows. It has no approved products and relies on a single manufacturer for its lead product candidate, posing significant operational risks.
  • Technological Obsolescence: The rapidly changing technology landscape in life sciences could render its products obsolete.
  • Global Economic Uncertainties: The company is vulnerable to global economic uncertainties, including trade tariffs and geopolitical tensions, which could adversely affect its business.
  • Regulatory Risks: Potential disruptions from changes in U.S. federal and state regulatory agencies could impact the company's ability to obtain FDA clearances or approvals for its products, hindering growth. Modifications to products may require new regulatory approvals, and failure to comply with ongoing FDA requirements could lead to product restrictions or market withdrawal.
  • Intellectual Property Risks: The company may face litigation costs and challenges in protecting proprietary information. Additionally, cybersecurity threats could disrupt operations and compromise sensitive data.
  • Financing Needs: Management acknowledges the need for additional financing to execute its business plan and fund operations. The company plans to seek funding through equity offerings, debt securities, and strategic partnerships, but there is no assurance that such financing will be available on reasonable terms.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in stock price and potential delisting from The Nasdaq Capital Market if it fails to maintain compliance with listing requirements. The concentration of ownership among executive officers and directors may also limit new investors' influence on corporate decisions.

SEC Filing: Autonomix Medical, Inc. [ AMIX ] - 10-K - May. 29, 2025
